what_it_does: This file is a noticeboard item that acts as a working design document container for the dragnet system; it records metadata and an ordered list of board item addresses that represent addressable, commentable discussion blocks (B0-B6), but does not include the block contents themselves.
observations: YAML front matter with id, address, type=document, source=claude_code, state=draft, title, author_session, channel, thread, links, and an order array of 16 board:// item addresses.
Body states it is a working design document for the dragnet and its parts, with B0 as a full session preamble and B1-B6 as the lead's last response verbatim.
The file contains no actual B0-B6 content; it only points to other items.
History array records state transitions: filed, sequence the blocks, insert earlier response, and short-lead title change.
standouts: It is an index/pointer document that sequences external board items rather than embedding the design discussion content.
The order array is the primary payload, defining a reading sequence for a multi-block response.
History shows iterative editorial restructuring of the discussion order and title.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML front matter and a markdown body.
Uses board:// URI addresses for cross-item references.
Maintains an order array and an edit history array with timestamps and notes.
concerns: The file claims to contain verbatim response blocks B0-B6, but only lists their addresses; the actual content is not present here.
As a design document, it contains no design substance in the body, only procedural description.
It remains in draft state with no links to related items beyond the order list.
notes: A draft noticeboard index that sequences sixteen addressable blocks for a dragnet design discussion, but holds only metadata and pointers rather than the block contents.
questions: Where are the actual B0-B6 block contents stored?
What does each ordered board item contain?
What is the dragnet system being designed?
Why is this item still in draft state?
How are comments intended to attach to individual blocks?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a working design document for the dragnet and its parts, but the actual file is an organizational index that points to blocks elsewhere; the design content is not present in this file.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to turn a design discussion into a structured, sequenced, commentable document by splitting a long response into addressable blocks and providing a top-level ordering and history.
novelty: It treats a threaded design conversation as a document made of ordered, separately-addressable board items, with a preamble and response blocks referenced by URI rather than embedded inline.
contribution: Provides a top-level organizational entry point and explicit reading order for a multi-block design discussion, preserving editorial history and enabling block-level referencing and commenting.
summary_for_embedding: Draft noticeboard item that indexes and sequences sixteen board items intended as addressable blocks of a dragnet design discussion.
intention_for_embedding: To organize a design conversation into an ordered, commentable set of blocks with a persistent editorial history.
